name: jd2005l--opencortex
description: Self-improving memory architecture for OpenClaw agents. Structured memory files, nightly distillation, weekly synthesis, enforced principles (P0 for custom, P1-P8 managed), write-ahead durability, and model-agnostic delegation — so your agent compounds knowledge instead of forgetting it. Includes opt-in metrics tracking with growth charts and compound scoring to measure effectiveness over time. All sensitive features (voice profiling, infrastructure auto-collection, git push) are OFF by default and require explicit opt-in via environment variable or flag. Safe to install: no network calls during setup, fully auditable bash scripts, isolated cron sessions scoped to workspace only. Use when: (1) setting up a new OpenClaw instance, (2) user asks to improve/organize memory, (3) user wants the agent to stop forgetting things, (4) bootstrapping a fresh agent with best practices. NOT for: runtime memory_search queries (use built-in memory tools). Triggers: "set up memory", "organize yourself", "stop forgetting", "memory architecture", "self-improving", "cortex", "bootstrap memory", "memory optimization".
